page: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v5 is an EDSv5-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores with hyper-threading, operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of RAM, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. It includes 2458 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the E64ds_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, including top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory performance is also strong, with high uncached read and write bandwidth for large blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the top 25 percent of tested systems, alongside high static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance metrics, such as single-threaded gzip compression and single-core stress-ng, fall into the average range.

The lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy parallel acceleration tasks. For instance, LLM inference text generation using a large 70B model is weak, yielding only 2.72 tokens per second, though prompt processing speeds remain average. Given the high memory density and local storage capacity, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving rather than large-scale AI model generation.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64ds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. It includes 2458 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, high uncached memory bandwidth, and strong database operations, as evidenced by its top-tier Redis SET and Passmark database benchmarks. Conversely, single-core performance and single-threaded operations remain average. Due to the absence of a GPU, the server exhibits weak performance in large-scale LLM text generation. The hardware configuration and benchmark profile make this server highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core CPU and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, designed for database and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-capacity local storage and strong multi-core performance for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB RAM providing a 1:8 resource ratio
2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Average single-core processing and weak large-scale LLM text generation
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, Redis deployments, and web serving
